title: impatiens atrosanguinea
intro: Impatiens atrosanguinea, also known as the bloody garden balsam, is a flowering plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Impatiens atrosanguinea include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent.
